compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Implemented + independently concurrency-reviewed (reviewer stress-tested 50x; no blockers). Map runs with bounded concurrency (max_concurrency from IR, default 1=serial): parallel agent calls, ALL commits+state writes serialized via rc.lock/_guard. Concurrency isolated in map_fanout.py (the sole narrow Burr-tripwire relaxation, recorded rationale); ban stays armed over executor/interpreter (lazy import keeps AST clean). Deterministic replay preserved (LWW per iteration-keyed frame marker). Tests: serial-parity, serialized-commits probe, REAL concurrency via 4-party barrier (would deadlock if serial), exactly-once replay under concurrency, guarded control-in-map-body. 1 MAJOR review finding (unguarded control-construct state writes — GIL-masked) fixed+regression-tested. 171 tests pass, ruff clean. Commit 4fab656f.

compiled_state.description:
## Why
Fan-out steps are I/O-bound LLM calls; serial execution wastes latency. Bounded concurrency is feasible in v1
with deterministic replay preserved, because iteration-keyed markers make map iterations order-independent.

## What
Execute map iterations with BOUNDED concurrency (parallel LLM calls), serializing event commits through the
existing store lock. Preserve deterministic replay. Relax the Burr tripwire NARROWLY for the map path only, with
a recorded rationale; the async/threading ban stays in force everywhere else.

## Scope
- src/rebar/llm/workflow/executor.py (map execution)
- tests/unit/workflow/test_executor_tripwire.py (scoped relaxation + rationale)
Out: parallelism outside the map path.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Map runs N agent steps concurrently with a configurable bound; event commits serialized.
- [ ] Deterministic replay holds under concurrency (order-independent via iteration markers).
- [ ] Tripwire permits bounded concurrency in the map path ONLY, with recorded rationale; ban intact elsewhere.
compiled_state.title: Bounded-concurrent fan-out (parallel map)
